en: Do not rebuke an older man but encourage him as you would a father. Treat younger men like brothers,
zh: 不要嚴厲責備老年人，卻要勸他好像勸父親；勸青年人好像勸弟兄；

en: older women like mothers, younger women like sisters, in all purity.
zh: 勸年老的婦人好像勸母親；存著純潔的心，勸青年女子好像勸姊妹。

en: Honor widows who are truly widows.
zh: 要敬重供養那些無依無靠的寡婦。

en: But if a widow has children or grandchildren, let them first learn to show godliness to their own household and to make some return to their parents, for this is pleasing in the sight of God.
zh: 寡婦若有兒孫，就應當讓兒孫先在自己家裡學習孝道，報答親恩，因為這在 神面前是蒙悅納的。

en: She who is truly a widow, left all alone, has set her hope on God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day,
zh: 那無依無靠獨居的寡婦，她們仰望 神，晝夜不住地祈求禱告。

en: but she who is self-indulgent is dead even while she lives.
zh: 寡婦若奢侈宴樂，雖然活著也是死的。

en: Command these things as well, so that they may be without reproach.
zh: 這些事你要囑咐她們，使她們無可指摘。

en: But if anyone does not provide for his relatives, and especially for members of his household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
zh: 人若不看顧自己的人，尤其不看顧自己一家的人，就是背棄信仰，比不信的人還壞。

en: Let a widow be enrolled if she is not less than sixty years of age, having been the wife of one husband,
zh: 寡婦登記，年紀必須到達六十歲，只作過一個丈夫的妻子，

en: and having a reputation for good works: if she has brought up children, has shown hospitality, has washed the feet of the saints, has cared for the afflicted, and has devoted herself to every good work.
zh: 並且要有善行的見證：就如養育兒女，接待客旅，替聖徒洗腳，救濟困苦的人，盡力行各樣的善事等。

en: But refuse to enroll younger widows, for when their passions draw them away from Christ, they desire to marry
zh: 至於年輕的寡婦，不要給她們登記，因為她們一旦情慾衝動而背棄基督的時候，就想結婚。

en: and so incur condemnation for having abandoned their former faith.
zh: 她們被定罪，是因為丟棄了起初的信誓。

en: Besides that, they learn to be idlers, going about from house to house, and not only idlers, but also gossips and busybodies, saying what they should not.
zh: 同時，她們懶惰慣了，挨家閒遊；不但懶惰，而且好說閒話，好管閒事，說不該說的話。

en: So I would have younger widows marry, bear children, manage their households, and give the adversary no occasion for slander.
zh: 因此，我願年輕的寡婦結婚，生兒育女，管理家務，不給敵人有辱罵的機會；

en: For some have already strayed after Satan.
zh: 因為有些人已經轉去跟從撒但了。

en: If any believing woman has relatives who are widows, let her care for them. Let the church not be burdened, so that it may care for those who are really widows.
zh: 如果信主的婦女家中有寡婦，她就應該救濟她們，不要讓教會受累。這樣，教會就可以救濟那些無依無靠的寡婦了。

en: Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in preaching and teaching.
zh: 那些善於治理教會的長老，尤其是那些在講道和教導上勞苦的長老，你們應當看他們是配受加倍的敬重和供奉的。

en: For the Scripture says, "You shall not muzzle an ox when it treads out the grain," and, "The laborer deserves his wages."
zh: 因為經上說：“牛踹穀的時候，不可籠住牠的嘴。”又說：“作工的配得工價。”

en: Do not admit a charge against an elder except on the evidence of two or three witnesses.
zh: 控訴長老的事，除非有兩三個證人，否則不要受理。

en: As for those who persist in sin, rebuke them in the presence of all, so that the rest may stand in fear.
zh: 常常犯罪的，你要當眾責備他們，使其餘的人也有所懼怕。

en: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus and of the elect angels I charge you to keep these rules without prejudging, doing nothing from partiality.
zh: 我在 神和基督耶穌以及蒙揀選的天使面前叮囑你，要毫無成見地持守這些話，行事也不要偏心。

en: Do not be hasty in the laying on of hands, nor take part in the sins of others; keep yourself pure.
zh: 不要匆匆忙忙地替別人按手，也不要在別人的罪上有分，要保守自己清潔。

en: (No longer drink only water, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.)
zh: 因為你的胃不好，而且身體常常軟弱，不要單單喝水，可以稍微用點酒。

en: The sins of some men are conspicuous, going before them to judgment, but the sins of others appear later.
zh: 有些人的罪是明顯的，就先受審判；有些人的罪是後來才顯露出來的。

en: So also good works are conspicuous, and even those that are not cannot remain hidden.
zh: 照樣，善行也是明顯的，就算不明顯，也不能隱藏。
